summary: An automated codebase discovery and synchronization system that builds a structured knowledge base using LLMs and vector search. It enables semantic understanding of large repositories through agentic exploration, incremental git-based syncing, and context-enriched indexing.

subsystems.name: Agentic Workflow
subsystems.description: Orchestrates the discovery pipeline using LangGraph, coordinating specialized agents for interviewing, exploring, analyzing, and indexing codebases.

subsystems.name: Knowledge & Storage
subsystems.description: Manages the structured representation of the codebase, including Pydantic models, semantic vector storage with LanceDB, and local embedding generation.

subsystems.name: LLM Infrastructure
subsystems.description: Provides a unified interface for multiple LLM providers with built-in caching, token tracking, and provider-specific optimizations.

subsystems.name: Sync Engine
subsystems.description: Handles incremental updates by comparing git states and applying 'fast-path' or 'accurate-path' updates to the knowledge base.

subsystems.name: CLI & UI
subsystems.description: Provides the interactive terminal interface, setup wizards, and session management.

subsystems.name: Agent Tools
subsystems.description: Standardized tools for agents to interact with the filesystem, execute commands, and communicate with the user.

coding_patterns.name: Budget-aware Batch Packing
coding_patterns.description: Optimizes LLM costs by packing multiple small file summaries into a single LLM request based on character count limits.
coding_patterns.when_to_use: When processing a large number of small items (files, logs, snippets) that need individual processing but can fit within a single context window.

code_snippets.label: Batch packing logic

coding_patterns.name: LangGraph State Reducer Pattern
coding_patterns.description: Uses Annotated types in TypedDict state definitions to specify how new data should be merged into the global graph state (e.g., replacing vs. appending).
coding_patterns.when_to_use: When building complex LangGraph workflows where different nodes update specific parts of a shared state object.

code_snippets.label: State schema with reducers
code_snippets.code:
class DiscoveryState(TypedDict):
    messages: Annotated[list[BaseMessage], add_messages]
    file_analyses: Annotated[dict[str, FileAnalysis], _merge_dicts]
    project_context: Annotated[str, _replace]
    subsystems: Annotated[list[Subsystem], _replace]

coding_patterns.name: Context-Header Enrichment
coding_patterns.description: Prepends AI-generated summaries and metadata as headers to raw code chunks before vector indexing to improve semantic retrieval accuracy.
coding_patterns.when_to_use: When indexing code for RAG where raw code might lack the semantic keywords needed for natural language queries.

code_snippets.label: Enrichment header generation

coding_patterns.name: Provider Registry with Lazy Imports
coding_patterns.description: A factory pattern that maps provider names to instantiation logic, using dynamic imports to avoid heavy dependency loading until a specific LLM is needed.
coding_patterns.when_to_use: When supporting multiple third-party integrations (LLMs, Databases) where users might only have one of the required libraries installed.

code_snippets.label: LLM Provider Registry

proprietary_tech.name: EnrichedChunk
proprietary_tech.description: A specialized data structure that pairs raw code segments with AI-generated context to facilitate high-precision semantic search in technical domains.

proprietary_tech.name: Incremental Sync Engine
proprietary_tech.description: A git-aware synchronization logic that minimizes LLM usage by detecting file changes via commit hashes and selectively updating the KnowledgeBase.

file_analyses.purpose: Updated the default model for OpenAI in the supported providers table.
Defines project metadata, dependencies, and CLI entry points for the failsafe package.

Implements an incremental synchronization engine that updates a vector store by comparing the current git state against a stored state, using LLM-driven summarization and a committed knowledge base to minimize processing costs.
Tracks and accumulates token usage across LLM calls in a thread-safe manner.

Manages the main interactive menu for selecting discovery and synchronization actions.

Initializes the tools subpackage for agents.

Defines and compiles the main LangGraph StateGraph for the discovery workflow pipeline.
Initializes the agents package for failsafe.
Synthesizes exploration results into a structured knowledge base using a mix of Python logic and LLM calls.
Traverses a codebase to generate structured per-file summaries using an LLM, employing budget-aware batching for small files and a map-reduce strategy for large files.
Builds a local vector store by enriching file content with AI-generated summaries and metadata.
Conducts a brief user interview to gather initial project context before exploration.
Initializes the state package for failsafe agents.

Builds enriched code chunks for vector indexing by combining AI-generated context headers with raw code to improve semantic retrieval.
Defines Pydantic schemas for structured codebase understanding, including subsystems, coding patterns, and file-level analyses.
Manages an in-memory query and access layer for the KnowledgeBase, providing semantic and keyword search, persistence, and formatted context generation for LLM agents.
Manages incremental updates to the vector store and knowledge base after files are modified, supporting both fast (agent-described) and accurate (LLM-summarized) paths.
Provides a local LanceDB-backed vector store for indexing and searching code chunks using semantic embeddings.

architecture_notes: The system follows a modular agentic architecture orchestrated by LangGraph. It transitions from an unstructured codebase to a structured KnowledgeBase (Pydantic models) through a linear pipeline: Interview -> Explorer -> Analyzer -> Indexer. Data persistence is hybrid, using LanceDB for high-dimensional vector embeddings of 'EnrichedChunks' and local JSON for the relational metadata of subsystems and patterns. A thread-safe singleton layer manages cross-cutting concerns like LLM response caching and token usage tracking.
